instructions.instruction: Every Illuminate Admin API call needs both Authorization and PubNub-Version headers, with the API key from ILLUMINATE_API_KEY env var
instructions.original_snippets: Authorization: <ILLUMINATE_API_KEY> PubNub-Version: 2026-02-09 Content-Type: application/json ... Get the API key from Service Integrations ... Store it in `ILLUMINATE_API_KEY` env var or your secrets manager
instructions.relevant_when: Making any HTTP request to admin-api.pubnub.com/v2/illuminate/*

instructions.instruction: All Illuminate calls go to admin-api.pubnub.com
instructions.original_snippets: POST /v2/illuminate/business-objects HTTP/1.1 Host: admin-api.pubnub.com
instructions.relevant_when: Setting the URL for an Illuminate API call

instructions.instruction: Build resources in dependency order: Business Object → Metric/Query → Decision → Dashboard
instructions.original_snippets: Resources are built in dependency order. Skipping or reordering breaks things
instructions.relevant_when: Designing a multi-resource Illuminate setup

instructions.instruction: Business Object JSONPath fields start with $.message.body. for the published payload, or $.message.userId / $.message.channel for scalar paths
instructions.original_snippets: **body** | `$.message.body` | Your published payload; append `.` / `[]` for nested app fields (e.g. `$.message.body.user_id`). ... **userId** | `$.message.userId` ... **channel** | `$.message.channel`
instructions.relevant_when: Defining BO fields against the normalized document Illuminate evaluates against

instructions.instruction: Decision POSTs MUST include activeFrom, activeUntil, hitType: 'SINGLE', and executeOnce: false — missing any returns 500 Internal Server Error
instructions.original_snippets: The following fields are **required** despite being documented as optional. Omitting any of them returns `500 Internal Server Error` ... `activeFrom` ... `activeUntil` ... `hitType` | Always `"SINGLE"` ... `executeOnce` | Always `false`
instructions.relevant_when: Creating any Illuminate Decision

instructions.instruction: Create a Decision via the 4-step workflow: POST with enabled:false rules:[], read auto-generated IDs, PUT with rules, PUT with enabled:true
instructions.original_snippets: The 4-step workflow is mandatory: POST with `rules: []` and `enabled: false`, READ auto-generated IDs, PUT with rules, PUT to enable. ... Step 1: POST with `enabled: false` and `rules: []` ... Step 2: Read the Response ... Step 3: PUT with Rules ... Step 4: PUT with `enabled: true`
instructions.relevant_when: Creating a new Decision (all source types)

instructions.instruction: Omit id on POST — IDs are auto-generated by the Admin API
instructions.original_snippets: Include all `inputFields`, `outputFields`, and `actions` **without `id` fields** — they are auto-generated. ... Omit `id` on POST — it is auto-generated.
instructions.relevant_when: Building the POST body for a Decision

instructions.instruction: On the PUT-with-rules step, every input field MUST appear in every rule's inputValues — use operation: 'ANY' for don't-care fields
instructions.original_snippets: **Every input field MUST appear in every rule's `inputValues`** — use `operation: "ANY"` for don't-care fields. ... `400: "Number of inputValues (N) does not match number of inputFields (M)"` | A rule's `inputValues` doesn't include every `inputField` | Add an entry (use `operation: "ANY"` for don't-care)
instructions.relevant_when: Building the rules array for a Decision PUT

instructions.instruction: Use ONCE_PER_INTERVAL_PER_CONDITION_GROUP for executionLimitType — ONCE_PER_INTERVAL_PER_CONDITION is invalid
instructions.original_snippets: `executionLimitType: "ONCE_PER_INTERVAL_PER_CONDITION"` is **not valid** despite appearing in some docs. Use `ONCE_PER_INTERVAL_PER_CONDITION_GROUP`. ... > **`ONCE_PER_INTERVAL_PER_CONDITION` is NOT valid.** It appears in some docs but the API rejects it.
instructions.relevant_when: Setting executionLimitType on a Decision action

instructions.instruction: Action objects use actionType (not type) and outputFields use variable + name (not type)
instructions.original_snippets: **Critical naming**: action objects use `"actionType"` (not `"type"`). `outputFields` use `"variable"` and `"name"` (not `"type"`). ... Action does nothing | Used `"type"` instead of `"actionType"` | Rename to `actionType`
instructions.relevant_when: Building actions or outputFields in a Decision body

instructions.instruction: BUSINESSOBJECT decisions require BOTH sourceId AND businessObjectId at the top level, set to the same BO ID
instructions.original_snippets: BUSINESSOBJECT decisions need **both** `sourceId` and `businessObjectId` set to the same BO ID. ... **Require both `"sourceId": "<bo-id>"` and `"businessObjectId": "<bo-id>"`** at the top level (set to the same BO ID). Omitting `sourceId` returns `400: "sourceId must be a UUID"`.
instructions.relevant_when: Creating a Decision sourced from a Business Object

instructions.instruction: BUSINESSOBJECT decisions: omit executionFrequency entirely — do not send null
instructions.original_snippets: For BUSINESSOBJECT decisions, **omit** `executionFrequency` entirely (don't send `null`). ... **Completely omit `executionFrequency` from PUT bodies** — sending `null` triggers a plan error. ... Plan error on BUSINESSOBJECT PUT | Sent `executionFrequency: null` | Omit the field entirely
instructions.relevant_when: Creating or updating a BUSINESSOBJECT Decision

instructions.instruction: BUSINESSOBJECT decisions use sourceType FIELD for input fields (not DIMENSION)
instructions.original_snippets: `inputFields` must use `"sourceType": "FIELD"` — **not** `"DIMENSION"`.
instructions.relevant_when: Defining inputFields on a BUSINESSOBJECT Decision

instructions.instruction: METRIC COUNT decisions use sourceType BUSINESSOBJECT for the count input field (sourceId = BO ID), since there is no measureId for COUNT metrics
instructions.original_snippets: | `METRIC` — count (COUNT/COUNT_DISTINCT) | One `BUSINESSOBJECT` field (`sourceId` = BO ID, `dataType: "NUMERIC"`) + one `DIMENSION` per dimension field | **Required** | ... Use `"sourceType": "BUSINESSOBJECT"` with `sourceId` = the BO ID for the count input field. (There is no `measureId` for COUNT metrics.)
instructions.relevant_when: Defining inputFields on a METRIC Decision using a COUNT/COUNT_DISTINCT metric

instructions.instruction: METRIC aggregation (SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX) decisions use sourceType MEASURE with sourceId equal to the metric's measureId
instructions.original_snippets: Use `"sourceType": "MEASURE"` with `sourceId` = the metric's `measureId` for the aggregated value. Use `"sourceType": "DIMENSION"` for each grouped dimension input field.
instructions.relevant_when: Defining inputFields on a METRIC Decision using a SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX metric

instructions.instruction: PUT is full replacement — always send the complete body, not a partial diff
instructions.original_snippets: Active resource updates use **full-replacement PUT** — always send the complete body ... PUT is always **full replacement** — include the complete body every time.
instructions.relevant_when: Updating any Illuminate resource

instructions.instruction: To change structural fields on an active Decision, PUT enabled:false, PUT structural changes, PUT enabled:true
instructions.original_snippets: Active Decisions are **immutable for structural fields** (`inputFields`, `outputFields`, `actions`). To change them: 1. PUT with `enabled: false`. 2. PUT with the structural changes. 3. PUT with `enabled: true`.
instructions.relevant_when: Editing an active Decision's structural fields

instructions.instruction: METRIC decisions are limited to 3 per account — check existing decisions before creating, and never delete without explicit user confirmation
instructions.original_snippets: | `METRIC` decisions | 3 per account | `400: "A business object cannot have more than 3 associated decisions."` | ... **Before creating a new METRIC or QUERY decision, list existing decisions of that type.** If at or near the limit, ask the user which existing decision to delete before retrying. **Never delete without explicit confirmation.**
instructions.relevant_when: Creating a new METRIC or QUERY Decision

instructions.instruction: Cascade deletes are permanent — confirm with the user before any DELETE, since deleting a BO removes all its dependent Metrics, Decisions, and Queries
instructions.original_snippets: Deleting a Business Object permanently deletes all dependent **Metrics**, **Decisions**, and **Queries**. ... All cascade deletes are permanent; require explicit user confirmation before any `DELETE`.
instructions.relevant_when: Performing any DELETE against Illuminate resources

instructions.instruction: Use template variables ${variable} or ${Input Field Name} in action bodies — manage_illuminate resolves these to UUIDs
instructions.original_snippets: Template variables support `${outputVariable}` and `${inputFieldName}` syntax. When using the `manage_illuminate` MCP tool's `create` operation, `${...}` references in action template bodies are automatically resolved from human-readable names to auto-generated UUIDs — use `${User ID}`, `${points}`, etc., instead of raw UUIDs.
instructions.relevant_when: Authoring action templates that reference Decision input or output fields

instructions.instruction: Use APPCONTEXT_SET_USER_METADATA / _CHANNEL_METADATA / _MEMBERSHIP_METADATA for App Context targets and PUBNUB_PUBLISH for publishing a message
instructions.original_snippets: | `PUBNUB_PUBLISH` | Publishes a message to a PubNub channel ... | `APPCONTEXT_SET_USER_METADATA` | Sets custom fields on a user object | | `APPCONTEXT_SET_CHANNEL_METADATA` | Sets custom fields on a channel object | | `APPCONTEXT_SET_MEMBERSHIP_METADATA` | Sets custom fields on a user-channel membership |
instructions.relevant_when: Choosing the actionType for a Decision

instructions.instruction: Decision evaluation windows for Metrics are limited to 60, 300, 600, 900, 1800, 3600, or 86400 seconds — other values are rejected
instructions.original_snippets: Evaluation windows for Metrics: only `60, 300, 600, 900, 1800, 3600, 86400` seconds
instructions.relevant_when: Choosing a Metric's evaluation window
